MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Order of Examination as to defendant/judgment debtor Daniel S. Lee, a/k/a Dan Lee.
Dept. 514. Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Jaime M. Hopps, CSR #13715. Telephone: 925-759-0770.

Lawrence D. Miller, Esq. appeared for plaintiff/judgment creditors David Lo, Daniel F.M. Lo and Alice M.C. Lo, who were not present for today's proceedings.
Defendant/judgment debtor Daniel S. Lee, a/k/a Dan Lee, was present in pro per, placed under oath and examined.
Proof of Service of OEX on file.

At the request of counsel, the case is called at 4:35 p.m. in order for counsel to place on the record the following:

(1) Defendant/judgment debtor Daniel S. Lee, a/k/a Dan Lee ("LEE") left the examination prior to its completion;

(2) Presently, LEE has approximately seventy-five thousand ($75,000.00) dollars on deposit in one or more banks in China;

(3) Plaintiffs/judgment creditors request an order from the Court enjoining LEE from transferring and/or withdrawing the $75,000.00 in question, unless the transfer and/or withdrawal is for partial payment on the outstanding balance of the judgment against LEE.

Good Cause Appearing, the Court GRANTS Plaintiffs' request, and Orders that LEE is enjoined from transferring and/or withdrawing the $75,000.00 from any account(s) he may have in banks in China.

LEE is ORDERED to transfer $75,000.00 to the account Lawrence D. Miller, counsel for plaintiffs, within twenty-four (24) hours of service of the written order (excluding weekends and holidays). Any funds transferred in excess of the actual judgment amount shall be returned to the LEE.

Order of Examination (OEX) is continued to November 9, 2017 at 2:00 p.m. in Department 514. Plaintiffs' counsel shall provide notice to LEE. Counsel shall prepare the Order After Hearing and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Proceeings Adjourned at 4:40 p.m.

Notice Of Motion To Expunge Plaintiffs' Notice Of Pendency Of Action
Off Calendar Jun-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DANIEL S. LEE AKA DAN LEE, DSL HOLDINGS, LLC AND JUNKO TANAKA'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF PENDENCY OF ACTION.
Appearances: Daniel A. Serot of Van de Poel Levy & Allen, LLP (291.8844) for defendants and moving parties; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. The seventh cause of action for fraudulent conveyance in the Second Amended Complaint is not a real property claim within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 405.4. Plaintiffs allege that defendant fraudulently transferred assets and money, not real property. The case law relied on by plaintiff is distinguishable from this action because that case law involved the fraudulent transfer of real property. Defendant's request for attorney's fees is denied because there was substantial justification for plaintiffs' position. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
